note: The git-anchored, MONOTONIC genesis state for the EvaluationContract attestation chain (feature 008, round 6). It is COMMITTED, git-reviewable state that the strict certify path loads and RUNS against — never a caller argument the scored party could forge. Fields: genesis_history_root is the REAL, non-empty history_root of the initial committed cohort (chain_root over its version content hashes) and is the immutable base of the history chain; latest.{history_root,attestation_root,evaluation_root} are the most recently certified roots and advance ONLY via contract.advance_committed_root(...), called by the harness after a successful strict certify. latest.evaluation_root (F2) is the committed root of the append-only EvaluationLedger: the strict certify path requires the presented prior_evaluations to reproduce + append-only-extend it (else EVALUATED_MORE_THAN_ONCE), so evaluate-once is COMMITTED monotonic state a caller cannot dodge with an empty ledger; its initial value is the reproducible empty-ledger root (chain_root([])). latest.exposure_root (R10-2) is the committed root of the append-only ExposureLedger, given the SAME committed-monotonic treatment: the presented exposure ledger must reproduce + append-only-extend it (else EXPOSURE_LEDGER_TRUNCATED), so dropping the incriminating curator record and re-signing can no longer certify; its initial value is the reproducible empty-ledger root. adjudicators (R10-6) is the committed adjudicator roster {id: {is_builder, is_curator}}: the certify path validates each precision verdict's self-asserted is_builder/is_curator against THIS roster and requires the adjudicator to be independent of the scored subject (else ADJUDICATOR_INVALID) — trust the committed roster, not the self-assertion. (In THIS repo A/B are the fixed test/smoke adjudicator identities; a production deployment commits the real independent adjudicators.) All committed roots here are chain_root/sha256 based; the R10-7 merkle domain-separation (CVE-2012-2459) changes only the dynamically-computed attestation/pool/sample roots, not these committed values. advance_committed_root now advances evaluation_root AND exposure_root alongside history/attestation. evaluator.id is the committed evaluator identity, and evaluator.verify_key_pub_hex is the committed ed25519 PUBLIC key that validate() verifies attestations against (F4). Only the PUBLIC key is committed: any repo reader can VERIFY an attestation but NONE can FORGE one, because the matching ed25519 PRIVATE signing key is held EXTERNALLY by a party that is NOT the scored subject (curator != subject) and is NEVER written to this file. Strict certification requires the presented history to append-only-EXTEND latest.history_root (else ATTESTATION_NOT_EXTENDING), the certified run to be bound to the terminal HEAD cohort (else DENOMINATOR_SHRINK, F1), the attestation to chain from latest.attestation_root (else GENESIS_UNANCHORED), and the attestation to be signed by the committed evaluator's ed25519 private key (verifying against verify_key_pub_hex) with the committed evaluator id (else ATTESTATION_INVALID). So no operator can silently truncate the cohort, re-anchor the chain to a fresh private genesis, re-score an already-evaluated blind set, or forge a signature: git history supplies the external timestamp and review a pure validator cannot. FINAL RESIDUAL (irreducible floor): (i) genesis-commit completeness — that what the curator committed here is itself complete — is git-reviewable, not validator-checkable; and (ii) ed25519 private-key custody — that the private signing key is held by a party that is NOT the scored subject — is organizational (curator != subject). In THIS repo the committed public key corresponds to a fixed test/smoke signing seed held in the test/build helpers (so the smoke and unit tests can produce honest attestations); a production deployment commits the curator's real public key while the real private key stays external. To rotate the genesis or advance the chain by hand (a deliberate, reviewable act) commit a new value here; the change is visible in git history.
